CohortPipe.description: Time-based and behavioral cohort analysis, retention, conversion, and LTV metrics.

form_time_cohorts.description: Form cohorts based on time periods
form_time_cohorts.usage_description: Groups users/customers by their sign-up, registration, or first activity date. Common use cases: tracking retention by sign-up month, analyzing quarterly cohorts, creating weekly purchase cohorts, and grouping customers by joining year for annual spending analysis. Supports daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly periods.

form_behavioral_cohorts.description: Form cohorts based on user behavior
form_behavioral_cohorts.usage_description: Creates user segments based on behavioral characteristics like spending levels, activity frequency, or feature usage. Useful for analyzing high-value vs low-value customers, power users vs casual users, and creating targeted marketing segments.

calculate_retention.usage_description: Measures how many users from each cohort return over time. Essential for understanding user engagement, product stickiness, and the effectiveness of onboarding processes. Supports classic, rolling, and unbounded retention calculations.

calculate_conversion.description: Calculate conversion funnel metrics for cohorts
calculate_conversion.usage_description: Tracks how users from different cohorts progress through conversion funnels (e.g., signup → activation → first purchase). Helps identify which cohorts have better conversion rates and where users drop off in the funnel.

calculate_lifetime_value.description: Calculate lifetime value metrics for cohorts
calculate_lifetime_value.usage_description: Estimates the total value each cohort will generate over time. Critical for customer acquisition cost analysis, marketing ROI calculations, and understanding which user segments are most valuable to the business.

FunnelPipe.description: User funnel analysis, including time, segment, and path analysis.

analyze_funnel.description: Analyze a user funnel using the existing CohortPipe framework
analyze_funnel.usage_description: Creates conversion funnels to track user progression through defined steps (e.g., view → add to cart → purchase). Essential for identifying drop-off points, optimizing user flows, and measuring funnel performance across different user segments.

analyze_funnel_by_time.description: Analyze funnel performance over time using the existing CohortPipe framework
analyze_funnel_by_time.usage_description: Tracks how funnel conversion rates change over time, helping identify seasonal patterns, the impact of product changes, and long-term trends in user behavior. Useful for measuring the success of optimization efforts.

analyze_funnel_by_segment.description: Analyze funnel performance across different user segments using the existing CohortPipe framework
analyze_funnel_by_segment.usage_description: Compares funnel performance across different user segments (e.g., by device, location, acquisition channel). Helps identify which segments have better conversion rates and where to focus optimization efforts.

analyze_user_paths.description: Analyze actual paths users take through the funnel (not just the ideal path)
analyze_user_paths.usage_description: Discovers the actual sequences of actions users take, not just the predefined funnel steps. Reveals unexpected user behaviors, alternative conversion paths, and opportunities for funnel optimization.

get_funnel_summary.description: Return a summary of funnel analysis results
get_funnel_summary.usage_description: Provides a comprehensive overview of funnel performance including conversion rates, drop-off points, and key metrics. Useful for reporting and identifying the most critical areas for improvement.

compare_segments.usage_description: Statistical comparison of funnel performance between different user segments. Helps identify significant differences in conversion rates and prioritize optimization efforts based on segment performance.

KMeansPipe.description: Pipeline-style K-means clustering with functional composition for unsupervised learning.

prepare_clustering_data.description: Prepare and preprocess data for K-means clustering with scaling and outlier handling
prepare_clustering_data.usage_description: Essential preprocessing step that scales features to similar ranges, handles outliers, and prepares data for clustering. Critical for ensuring clustering quality and preventing features with larger scales from dominating the analysis.

engineer_clustering_features.description: Create advanced features optimized for clustering algorithms
engineer_clustering_features.usage_description: Creates interaction features, polynomial features, ratios, and aggregations that can improve clustering quality. Useful for capturing complex relationships in the data and creating more meaningful customer segments.

reduce_dimensions.description: Apply dimensionality reduction for visualization and clustering efficiency
reduce_dimensions.usage_description: Reduces the number of features using PCA or t-SNE to improve clustering performance and enable 2D/3D visualization. Essential for high-dimensional data and creating interpretable cluster visualizations.

configure_kmeans.usage_description: Sets up K-means parameters including number of clusters, initialization method, and convergence criteria. The core configuration step for customer segmentation, market research, and data exploration.

configure_minibatch_kmeans.description: Configure MiniBatch K-means for large-scale clustering
configure_minibatch_kmeans.usage_description: Optimized version of K-means for large datasets that processes data in mini-batches. Useful for big data applications where memory efficiency and speed are critical.

fit_kmeans.description: Train K-means model and assign cluster labels
fit_kmeans.usage_description: Executes the clustering algorithm and assigns each data point to a cluster. The main training step that creates the customer segments or data groupings.

evaluate_clustering.usage_description: Measures clustering quality using metrics like silhouette score, Calinski-Harabasz score, and Davies-Bouldin score. Essential for validating cluster quality and comparing different clustering configurations.

profile_clusters.description: Create detailed statistical profiles of each cluster
profile_clusters.usage_description: Analyzes the characteristics of each cluster to understand what makes them distinct. Critical for interpreting clusters and creating actionable business insights from the segmentation.

find_optimal_k.description: Determine optimal k using elbow method and silhouette analysis
find_optimal_k.usage_description: Automatically determines the best number of clusters using statistical methods. Eliminates guesswork in choosing the number of segments and ensures optimal clustering quality.

plot_clusters_2d.description: Visualize clusters in 2D feature space with centroids
plot_clusters_2d.usage_description: Creates scatter plots showing how data points are grouped in 2D space. Essential for understanding cluster boundaries, identifying outliers, and communicating results to stakeholders.

plot_cluster_profiles.usage_description: Creates heatmaps showing how each cluster differs across features. Useful for understanding cluster characteristics and identifying the most important features for each segment.

plot_elbow_curve.description: Plot elbow curve for optimal k selection using within-cluster sum of squares
plot_elbow_curve.usage_description: Visualizes the trade-off between number of clusters and clustering quality. Helps in manually selecting the optimal number of clusters when automatic methods are inconclusive.

plot_silhouette_scores.description: Plot silhouette scores across different k values for cluster quality assessment
plot_silhouette_scores.usage_description: Shows how well-separated the clusters are for different values of k. Higher silhouette scores indicate better-defined, more separated clusters.

save_kmeans_model.description: Save trained clustering model with preprocessing pipeline and cluster profiles
save_kmeans_model.usage_description: Persists the trained model for future use, including preprocessing steps and cluster profiles. Essential for production deployment and model versioning.

print_cluster_summary.description: Print comprehensive clustering analysis summary and insights
print_cluster_summary.usage_description: Provides a complete overview of the clustering results including cluster sizes, key characteristics, and business insights. Useful for reporting and stakeholder communication.

MovingAggrPipe.description: Moving window and expanding window metrics, including moving averages, variance, quantiles, and custom aggregations.

moving_average.description: Calculate moving averages for specified columns
moving_average.usage_description: Smooths time series data to identify trends and reduce noise. Common applications: smoothing daily sales data, analyzing website traffic trends, and identifying patterns in sensor readings. Supports simple, weighted, and exponential moving averages.

moving_variance.description: Calculate moving variance and standard deviation for specified columns
moving_variance.usage_description: Measures volatility and variability over time. Useful for detecting changes in data variability, analyzing risk in financial data, and identifying periods of unusual activity in sensor data.

moving_sum.description: Calculate moving sums for specified columns
moving_sum.usage_description: Tracks cumulative totals over rolling windows. Common uses: calculating rolling revenue totals, tracking cumulative user activity, and analyzing running totals in time series data.

moving_quantile.description: Calculate moving quantiles (percentiles) for specified columns
moving_quantile.usage_description: Provides robust measures of central tendency and spread that are less sensitive to outliers than means. Useful for analyzing data distributions over time and identifying changes in data characteristics.

moving_correlation.description: Calculate moving correlations between pairs of columns
moving_correlation.usage_description: Tracks how relationships between variables change over time. Essential for understanding dynamic relationships in financial data, sensor networks, and multivariate time series analysis.

moving_zscore.description: Calculate moving z-scores for specified columns
moving_zscore.usage_description: Standardizes data relative to local windows, making it easier to identify unusual values. Useful for anomaly detection, quality control, and identifying outliers in streaming data.

moving_apply_by_group.description: Apply a function by group within a moving window
moving_apply_by_group.usage_description: Performs group-specific calculations within moving windows. Useful for analyzing panel data where you need to maintain group structure while applying time-based calculations.

moving_ratio.description: Calculate the ratio of sums of two columns within a moving window
moving_ratio.usage_description: Tracks ratios over time, such as conversion rates, efficiency metrics, or performance ratios. Useful for analyzing trends in proportional relationships.

detect_turning_points.description: Detect peaks and troughs (turning points) in time series data
detect_turning_points.usage_description: Identifies local maxima and minima in time series data. Essential for identifying trend reversals, seasonal peaks, and critical points in business metrics.

moving_regression.description: Calculate rolling regression coefficients and statistics
moving_regression.usage_description: Tracks how relationships between variables evolve over time. Useful for analyzing changing market dynamics, evolving customer behavior patterns, and dynamic system modeling.

moving_min_max.description: Calculate moving minimum and maximum values for specified columns
moving_min_max.usage_description: Tracks the range of values over time. Useful for monitoring data quality, identifying extreme values, and analyzing the spread of data distributions.

moving_count.description: Calculate moving count of observations or observations meeting a condition
moving_count.usage_description: Tracks frequency of events or conditions over time. Useful for analyzing event patterns, counting occurrences within windows, and monitoring activity levels.

moving_aggregate.description: Apply a custom aggregation function over a moving window
moving_aggregate.usage_description: Allows application of any custom function to moving windows. Provides flexibility for domain-specific calculations and specialized time series analysis.

moving_percentile_rank.description: Calculate the percentile rank of each value within a moving window
moving_percentile_rank.usage_description: Ranks values relative to their local context. Useful for identifying relative performance, ranking within peer groups, and understanding competitive positioning over time.

time_weighted_average.description: Calculate time-weighted average with exponential decay for specified columns
time_weighted_average.usage_description: Gives more weight to recent observations. Useful for emphasizing recent trends, creating responsive indicators, and analyzing time-sensitive metrics.

moving_cumulative.description: Calculate moving cumulative metrics for specified columns
moving_cumulative.usage_description: Tracks running totals within moving windows. Useful for analyzing cumulative effects, tracking progress over time, and understanding accumulation patterns.

expanding_window.description: Calculate metrics using an expanding window (all prior data)
expanding_window.usage_description: Uses all available historical data for each calculation. Useful for creating benchmarks, understanding long-term trends, and analyzing cumulative performance.

OperationsPipe.description: Statistical and comparison operations, including percent/absolute change, covariate adjustment, filtering, and power analysis.

PercentChange.description: Compute the percent change (other - baseline) / baseline
PercentChange.usage_description: Calculates percentage differences between groups or time periods. Essential for A/B testing, measuring lift from experiments, comparing performance across segments, and analyzing growth rates. Common use cases: conversion rate improvements, revenue growth analysis, and performance comparisons.

AbsoluteChange.usage_description: Measures absolute differences between groups or time periods. Useful for understanding the magnitude of changes, analyzing absolute improvements, and comparing raw performance differences. Often used alongside percent change for complete analysis.

MH.usage_description: Advanced statistical method for analyzing stratified data and controlling for confounding variables. Essential for complex experimental designs, epidemiological studies, and situations where you need to account for multiple factors simultaneously.

CUPED.description: Compute the absolute change adjusted using the CUPED approach
CUPED.usage_description: Reduces variance in experimental analysis by using pre-treatment covariates. Improves statistical power and precision in A/B testing, making it easier to detect true treatment effects and reducing the required sample size.

PrePostChange.description: Compute the percent change adjusted using the PrePost approach
PrePostChange.usage_description: Analyzes changes using pre-treatment data as covariates. Useful for before-after studies, measuring the impact of interventions, and analyzing changes while controlling for baseline differences.

FilterConditions.description: Filter the data to include only specified conditions
FilterConditions.usage_description: Subsets data to focus on specific groups or conditions. Essential for targeted analysis, comparing specific segments, and ensuring analysis focuses on relevant data subsets.

PowerAnalysis.description: Perform power analysis to determine sample size requirements
PowerAnalysis.usage_description: Calculates required sample sizes for statistical tests. Critical for experimental design, ensuring studies have sufficient power to detect meaningful effects, and optimizing resource allocation in research and testing.

StratifiedSummary.description: Create a summary of metrics stratified by specified columns
StratifiedSummary.usage_description: Provides detailed breakdowns of metrics across different groups or strata. Useful for understanding how performance varies across segments, identifying opportunities, and creating comprehensive reports.

BootstrapCI.description: Calculate bootstrap confidence intervals for the difference in means
BootstrapCI.usage_description: Provides robust confidence intervals without assuming specific data distributions. Essential for reliable statistical inference, especially with non-normal data or complex sampling designs.

MultiComparisonAdjustment.description: Apply multiple comparison adjustment to p-values
MultiComparisonAdjustment.usage_description: Controls for multiple testing to prevent false positive results. Critical when conducting multiple statistical tests, ensuring valid inference and maintaining appropriate significance levels.

ExecuteOperations.description: Execute the operations pipeline and return all calculated operations
ExecuteOperations.usage_description: Runs all configured operations and returns comprehensive results. Essential for obtaining final analysis results, creating reports, and accessing all calculated metrics and comparisons.

MetricsPipe.description: Basic, derived, statistical, and time series metrics, including groupby, filter, and display functions.

Count.description: Count the number of non-null entries in a column
Count.usage_description: Fundamental metric for counting records, users, transactions, or events. Essential for understanding data volume, tracking activity levels, and measuring frequency of occurrences. Common applications: counting daily active users, transaction volumes, and event frequencies.

Sum.description: Calculate the sum of a column
Sum.usage_description: Aggregates numeric values to understand totals and cumulative amounts. Critical for revenue analysis, cost calculations, and understanding total impact or volume. Used extensively in financial analysis and performance measurement.

Max.description: Calculate the maximum value of a column
Max.usage_description: Identifies peak values and upper bounds in data. Useful for understanding performance ceilings, identifying outliers, and analyzing extreme values. Common in performance analysis and outlier detection.

Min.description: Calculate the minimum value of a column
Min.usage_description: Identifies lowest values and lower bounds in data. Useful for understanding performance floors, identifying outliers, and analyzing extreme values. Often used alongside Max for range analysis.

Ratio.description: Calculate the ratio of sums of two columns
Ratio.usage_description: Creates proportional relationships between different metrics. Essential for efficiency metrics, conversion rates, and performance ratios. Common applications: conversion rates, efficiency ratios, and performance indicators.

Dot.description: Calculate the dot product between two columns
Dot.usage_description: Measures similarity and correlation between variables. Useful for similarity analysis, feature engineering, and understanding relationships between different metrics or features.

Nth.description: Get the nth value of a column after sorting by another column
Nth.usage_description: Retrieves specific ranked values from ordered data. Useful for finding top performers, identifying specific percentiles, and analyzing ranked data. Common in competitive analysis and performance ranking.

Variance.description: Calculate the variance of a column
Variance.usage_description: Measures data spread and variability. Essential for understanding data consistency, quality control, and risk assessment. Critical for statistical analysis and understanding data distributions.

StandardDeviation.description: Calculate the standard deviation of a column
StandardDeviation.usage_description: Measures data spread in the same units as the original data. More interpretable than variance for understanding variability and consistency. Essential for quality control and statistical analysis.

CV.description: Calculate the coefficient of variation of a column
CV.usage_description: Standardized measure of variability relative to the mean. Useful for comparing variability across different scales and understanding relative consistency. Common in quality control and comparative analysis.

Correlation.description: Calculate the Pearson correlation between two columns
Correlation.usage_description: Measures linear relationships between variables. Essential for understanding associations, feature relationships, and identifying potential causal connections. Fundamental for exploratory data analysis.

Cov.description: Calculate the covariance between two columns
Cov.usage_description: Measures how variables change together. Useful for understanding joint variability and building covariance matrices for multivariate analysis. Important for portfolio analysis and multivariate statistics.

Mean.description: Calculate the mean of a column
Mean.usage_description: Most common measure of central tendency. Essential for understanding typical values, creating benchmarks, and comparing performance across groups. Fundamental metric used in almost all analyses.

Median.description: Calculate the median of a column
Median.usage_description: Robust measure of central tendency that's less sensitive to outliers than the mean. Useful for skewed data, outlier-prone metrics, and understanding typical values in non-normal distributions.

Percentile.description: Calculate the specified percentile of a column
Percentile.usage_description: Provides flexible measures of data position and distribution. Essential for understanding data distributions, setting thresholds, and analyzing performance at specific points in the distribution.

PivotTable.description: Create a pivot table from the data
PivotTable.usage_description: Organizes data into cross-tabulated format for easy analysis. Essential for comparing metrics across different dimensions, creating summary reports, and identifying patterns in multi-dimensional data.

GroupBy.description: Group the data and apply aggregation functions
GroupBy.usage_description: Enables analysis by categories, segments, or dimensions. Critical for segment analysis, comparative studies, and understanding how metrics vary across different groups or conditions.

Filter.description: Filter the dataframe based on a condition
Filter.usage_description: Subsets data to focus on specific records or conditions. Essential for targeted analysis, removing irrelevant data, and ensuring analysis focuses on the right data subset.

CumulativeSum.description: Calculate the cumulative sum of a column
CumulativeSum.usage_description: Tracks running totals over time or sequence. Useful for understanding cumulative performance, tracking progress, and analyzing accumulation patterns in time series or ordered data.

RollingMetric.description: Calculate a rolling metric on a column
RollingMetric.usage_description: Applies functions over moving windows to smooth data and identify trends. Essential for time series analysis, trend identification, and reducing noise in sequential data.

Execute.description: Execute the pipeline and return all calculated metrics
Execute.usage_description: Runs all configured metrics and returns comprehensive results. Essential for obtaining final analysis results, creating reports, and accessing all calculated metrics in a structured format.

ShowPivot.description: Show a specific pivot table or the last created one
ShowPivot.usage_description: Displays pivot table results for analysis and reporting. Useful for reviewing cross-tabulated data, identifying patterns, and creating summary views of complex data relationships.

ShowDataFrame.usage_description: Displays the current state of the data for inspection and verification. Essential for data exploration, debugging analysis steps, and understanding how transformations affect the data.

ProphetPipe.description: Pipeline-style Prophet time series forecasting with functional composition.

prepare_prophet_data.description: Convert data to Prophet's required ds/y format with date validation
prepare_prophet_data.usage_description: Essential preprocessing step that converts data to Prophet's required format with 'ds' (date) and 'y' (value) columns. Critical for ensuring Prophet can properly handle the time series data and date formats.

add_regressors.description: Add external variables that influence the forecast
add_regressors.usage_description: Incorporates additional variables that affect the forecast (e.g., marketing spend, holidays, weather). Essential for improving forecast accuracy by accounting for external factors that influence the target variable.

add_holidays.description: Add holiday effects with custom or built-in holiday calendars
add_holidays.usage_description: Accounts for holiday effects that can significantly impact time series data. Critical for retail, e-commerce, and any business affected by seasonal events and holidays.

configure_prophet.description: Configure Prophet model parameters and seasonality settings
configure_prophet.usage_description: Sets up Prophet's core parameters including growth models, seasonality modes, and changepoint settings. Essential for optimizing forecast performance and capturing the right patterns in the data.

add_custom_seasonality.description: Add custom seasonal patterns beyond default yearly/weekly/daily
add_custom_seasonality.usage_description: Captures business-specific seasonal patterns that don't follow standard calendar cycles. Useful for industries with unique seasonality patterns or business cycles.

fit_prophet.description: Train the Prophet model on historical data
fit_prophet.usage_description: Executes the model training process using historical data. The core step that learns patterns, seasonality, and trends from the data to enable forecasting.

make_forecast.description: Generate future forecasts for specified periods
make_forecast.usage_description: Creates predictions for future time periods. Essential for planning, resource allocation, and understanding expected future performance based on historical patterns.

forecast_with_regressors.description: Generate forecasts with future values for external regressors
forecast_with_regressors.usage_description: Creates forecasts that incorporate future values of external variables. Critical for scenarios where you have planned values for regressors (e.g., planned marketing spend, known future events).

cross_validate_model.description: Evaluate model performance using time series cross-validation
cross_validate_model.usage_description: Assesses forecast accuracy using historical data to validate model performance. Essential for understanding forecast reliability and comparing different model configurations.

calculate_forecast_metrics.description: Calculate forecast accuracy metrics (MAE, RMSE, MAPE, coverage)
calculate_forecast_metrics.usage_description: Provides quantitative measures of forecast accuracy. Critical for model validation, comparing forecasting methods, and understanding the reliability of predictions.

print_forecast_metrics.usage_description: Displays forecast performance metrics in a readable format. Useful for reporting, model comparison, and communicating forecast accuracy to stakeholders.

plot_forecast.description: Visualize forecast results and decomposed components
plot_forecast.usage_description: Creates visualizations showing forecasts, confidence intervals, and trend/seasonal components. Essential for understanding forecast patterns and communicating results to stakeholders.

plot_cross_validation.description: Visualize cross-validation performance across different horizons
plot_cross_validation.usage_description: Shows how forecast accuracy varies across different prediction horizons. Useful for understanding model performance at different time scales and identifying optimal forecast horizons.

save_prophet_model.description: Save trained Prophet model and configuration to disk
save_prophet_model.usage_description: Persists the trained model for future use and deployment. Essential for production systems, model versioning, and avoiding retraining costs.

hyperparameter_tuning.description: Optimize Prophet hyperparameters using cross-validation grid search
hyperparameter_tuning.usage_description: Automatically finds optimal model parameters to maximize forecast accuracy. Critical for achieving the best possible forecast performance and reducing manual parameter tuning effort.

RFPipe.description: Pipeline-style Random Forest classifier with functional composition.

generate_numerical_features.description: Generate numerical features using mathematical transformations
generate_numerical_features.usage_description: Creates new features through mathematical transformations (log, sqrt, square, reciprocal) to improve model performance. Essential for feature engineering and capturing non-linear relationships in the data.

generate_categorical_features.description: Encode categorical features using various encoding methods
generate_categorical_features.usage_description: Converts categorical variables into numerical format using one-hot, label, or target encoding. Critical for including categorical data in machine learning models and handling high-cardinality categories.

generate_interaction_features.description: Create interaction features between column pairs
generate_interaction_features.usage_description: Creates new features that capture relationships between variables (multiplication, addition, subtraction, division). Useful for discovering complex patterns and improving model performance.

create_binary_labels.description: Create binary classification labels based on conditions
create_binary_labels.usage_description: Converts continuous variables into binary classification targets using threshold conditions. Essential for binary classification problems like churn prediction, fraud detection, and binary outcome modeling.

create_multiclass_labels.description: Create multiclass labels by binning continuous variables
create_multiclass_labels.usage_description: Converts continuous variables into multiple categories for multiclass classification. Useful for customer segmentation, risk categorization, and multi-level outcome prediction.

select_features.description: Select top k features using statistical tests
select_features.usage_description: Identifies the most important features for the model using statistical methods. Essential for reducing dimensionality, improving model interpretability, and focusing on the most predictive variables.

train_random_forest.description: Train a Random Forest classifier with specified parameters
train_random_forest.usage_description: Executes the Random Forest training process with configurable parameters. The core step that builds the ensemble model for classification or regression tasks.

save_model.description: Save trained model to disk using joblib
save_model.usage_description: Persists the trained model for future use and deployment. Essential for production systems, model versioning, and avoiding retraining costs.

predict.usage_description: Applies the trained model to new data to generate predictions. Essential for model deployment, scoring new records, and using the model for decision-making.

calculate_metrics.description: Calculate classification metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, f1)
calculate_metrics.usage_description: Evaluates model performance using standard classification metrics. Critical for understanding model quality, comparing different models, and ensuring adequate performance for deployment.

print_metrics.description: Print formatted classification metrics and feature importance
print_metrics.usage_description: Displays model performance and feature importance in a readable format. Useful for reporting, model comparison, and understanding which features drive predictions.

RiskPipe.description: Risk analysis, distribution fitting, VaR, CVaR, portfolio risk, simulation, and stress testing.

fit_distribution.description: Fit a probability distribution to data using maximum likelihood estimation
fit_distribution.usage_description: Identifies the best-fitting probability distribution for risk modeling. Essential for understanding data characteristics, creating risk models, and enabling probabilistic analysis of outcomes.

calculate_var.description: Calculate Value at Risk (VaR) for specified columns
calculate_var.usage_description: Measures potential losses at a given confidence level. Critical for financial risk management, portfolio analysis, and understanding downside risk in investment or business scenarios.

calculate_cvar.description: Calculate Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR/Expected Shortfall)
calculate_cvar.usage_description: Measures expected losses beyond the VaR threshold. Provides more comprehensive risk assessment than VaR alone, especially for tail risk analysis and extreme loss scenarios.

calculate_portfolio_risk.usage_description: Assesses risk at the portfolio level considering correlations and diversification effects. Essential for portfolio management, asset allocation, and understanding overall risk exposure.

monte_carlo_simulation.description: Perform Monte Carlo simulation for risk assessment
monte_carlo_simulation.usage_description: Generates multiple scenarios to assess risk under uncertainty. Critical for complex risk modeling, stress testing, and understanding the range of possible outcomes.

stress_test.description: Perform stress testing with specified scenarios
stress_test.usage_description: Evaluates performance under extreme or adverse conditions. Essential for risk management, regulatory compliance, and understanding vulnerability to specific risk factors.

rolling_risk_metrics.description: Calculate rolling risk metrics over time
rolling_risk_metrics.usage_description: Tracks how risk measures change over time. Useful for monitoring risk trends, identifying periods of increased risk, and understanding dynamic risk characteristics.

correlation_analysis.description: Perform correlation analysis for risk assessment
correlation_analysis.usage_description: Analyzes relationships between risk factors and variables. Essential for understanding risk drivers, diversification benefits, and identifying correlated risk exposures.

risk_attribution.usage_description: Decomposes total risk into contributions from individual factors. Critical for understanding risk sources, optimizing risk allocation, and identifying key risk drivers.

compare_distributions.description: Compare multiple distribution fits and select the best one
compare_distributions.usage_description: Evaluates different probability distributions to find the best fit. Essential for accurate risk modeling and ensuring appropriate distribution assumptions for risk calculations.

get_risk_summary.description: Get a summary of all risk analysis results
get_risk_summary.usage_description: Provides comprehensive overview of risk metrics and analysis results. Essential for reporting, decision-making, and communicating risk assessment to stakeholders.

SegmentationPipe.description: Segmentation and clustering analysis, including KMeans, DBSCAN, hierarchical, and rule-based segmentation.

get_features.description: Extract features from dataframe for segmentation
get_features.usage_description: Prepares and selects relevant features for customer or data segmentation. Essential for ensuring segmentation is based on meaningful characteristics and improving clustering quality.

run_kmeans.usage_description: Creates customer segments using K-means clustering algorithm. Most common segmentation method, useful for creating distinct, well-separated customer groups based on behavioral or demographic characteristics.

run_dbscan.usage_description: Creates segments using density-based clustering that can identify irregularly shaped clusters. Useful for discovering natural groupings in data and handling outliers effectively.

run_hierarchical.usage_description: Creates a hierarchical structure of segments that can be analyzed at different levels of granularity. Useful for understanding segment relationships and creating multi-level segmentation strategies.

run_rule_based.usage_description: Creates segments based on predefined business rules and criteria. Useful for implementing business logic, regulatory requirements, or specific segmentation strategies that require explicit rules.

generate_summary.usage_description: Provides comprehensive statistics and characteristics for each segment. Essential for understanding segment profiles, identifying key differences, and creating actionable insights from segmentation.

get_segment_data.description: Get data for a specific segment
get_segment_data.usage_description: Extracts data for individual segments for detailed analysis. Useful for segment-specific analysis, targeted marketing strategies, and understanding segment-specific behaviors.

compare_algorithms.usage_description: Evaluates different clustering methods to find the best approach for the data. Essential for choosing the most appropriate segmentation method and ensuring optimal results.

custom_calculation.description: Apply a custom calculation or transformation to the dataframe
custom_calculation.usage_description: Allows application of custom logic or transformations specific to business needs. Provides flexibility for domain-specific calculations and specialized segmentation requirements.

TimeSeriesPipe.description: Time series analysis, including lag/lead, variance, distribution, rolling window, and custom calculations.

lead.description: Create lead (future) values for specified columns.
lead.usage_description: Shifts data forward to access future values for analysis. Essential for time series forecasting, analyzing leading indicators, and understanding how current values relate to future outcomes.

lag.description: Create lag (past) values for specified columns.
lag.usage_description: Shifts data backward to access historical values. Critical for time series analysis, identifying autoregressive patterns, and understanding how past values influence current outcomes.

variance_analysis.description: Calculate variance and standard deviation for time series data.
variance_analysis.usage_description: Measures volatility and variability over time in time series data. Essential for understanding data stability, identifying periods of high volatility, and analyzing risk in time-dependent data.

distribution_analysis.description: Calculate distribution statistics and histograms for time series data.
distribution_analysis.usage_description: Analyzes the statistical distribution of time series data. Useful for understanding data characteristics, identifying distribution changes over time, and ensuring appropriate modeling assumptions.

cumulative_distribution.description: Calculate cumulative distribution (empirical CDF) for specified columns.
cumulative_distribution.usage_description: Creates cumulative distribution functions for time series data. Useful for understanding data percentiles, setting thresholds, and analyzing the full distribution of time series values.

rolling_window.description: Apply rolling window operations to time series data.
rolling_window.usage_description: Applies functions over moving time windows to smooth data and identify trends. Essential for trend analysis, noise reduction, and understanding patterns in time series data.

get_distribution_summary.description: Return a formatted summary of distribution analysis results
get_distribution_summary.usage_description: Provides comprehensive summary of time series distribution characteristics. Useful for reporting, understanding data properties, and communicating distribution analysis results.

custom_calculation.description: Apply a custom calculation function to the data
custom_calculation.usage_description: Allows application of custom time series calculations specific to business needs. Provides flexibility for domain-specific analysis and specialized time series transformations.

TrendPipe.description: Trend analysis, aggregation, growth rates, moving averages, decomposition, forecasting, and period comparison.

aggregate_by_time.usage_description: Groups and summarizes data by time periods (daily, weekly, monthly, etc.). Essential for trend analysis, reducing noise in time series data, and creating time-based summaries for analysis.

calculate_growth_rates.description: Calculate growth rates for aggregated metrics
calculate_growth_rates.usage_description: Measures how metrics change over time using percentage, logarithmic, or CAGR methods. Critical for understanding business growth, comparing performance across periods, and identifying growth trends.

calculate_moving_average.description: Calculate moving averages for aggregated metrics
calculate_moving_average.usage_description: Smooths time series data to identify underlying trends. Essential for trend analysis, reducing seasonal noise, and understanding long-term patterns in business metrics.

decompose_trend.description: Decompose time series into trend, seasonal, and residual components
decompose_trend.usage_description: Breaks down time series into trend, seasonal, and random components. Critical for understanding the underlying structure of time series data and identifying different types of patterns.

forecast_metric.description: Forecast future values of a metric
forecast_metric.usage_description: Predicts future values using various forecasting methods. Essential for planning, resource allocation, and understanding expected future performance based on historical patterns.

calculate_statistical_trend.usage_description: Tests whether observed trends are statistically significant. Critical for validating trend analysis, ensuring trends are not due to random variation, and making data-driven decisions.

compare_periods.description: Compare metrics between different time periods (e.g., year-over-year, month-over-month)
compare_periods.usage_description: Analyzes performance changes between comparable time periods. Essential for understanding seasonal patterns, measuring improvement over time, and identifying period-to-period changes.

get_top_metrics.description: Get top metrics based on growth, trend, or other criteria
get_top_metrics.usage_description: Identifies the best-performing metrics based on various criteria. Useful for focusing attention on the most important trends, identifying opportunities, and prioritizing analysis efforts.

AnomalyPipe.description: Anomaly and outlier detection using statistical, contextual, and collective methods, plus summaries and batch detection.

detect_statistical_outliers.usage_description: Identifies unusual values using statistical methods like z-score, IQR, and modified z-score. Essential for data quality control, fraud detection, and identifying unusual patterns in sensor data, financial transactions, or business metrics.

detect_contextual_anomalies.description: Detect contextual anomalies by comparing actual values with expected values
detect_contextual_anomalies.usage_description: Finds anomalies by comparing actual values to expected values based on time, seasonality, or other contextual factors. Critical for detecting unusual patterns in time series data, website traffic anomalies, and business performance outliers.

detect_collective_anomalies.description: Detect collective anomalies using machine learning methods on multiple features
detect_collective_anomalies.usage_description: Identifies unusual patterns across multiple variables using machine learning algorithms. Essential for complex anomaly detection, fraud detection, and identifying unusual behavior patterns that span multiple dimensions.

calculate_seasonal_residuals.description: Calculate residuals after removing seasonal patterns
calculate_seasonal_residuals.usage_description: Removes seasonal effects to focus on unusual patterns in the remaining data. Useful for detecting anomalies in seasonal data, understanding non-seasonal patterns, and improving anomaly detection accuracy.

detect_anomalies_from_residuals.usage_description: Identifies anomalies in residual data after seasonal or trend components have been removed. Useful for detecting unusual patterns in detrended data and improving anomaly detection precision.

get_anomaly_summary.description: Generate a summary of detected anomalies
get_anomaly_summary.usage_description: Provides comprehensive overview of detected anomalies including counts, characteristics, and patterns. Essential for understanding anomaly patterns, reporting findings, and prioritizing investigation efforts.

get_top_anomalies.description: Get the top n anomalies by score
get_top_anomalies.usage_description: Identifies the most significant anomalies based on anomaly scores. Useful for prioritizing investigation efforts, focusing on the most important outliers, and managing large numbers of detected anomalies.

detect_change_points.description: Detect change points in time series data
detect_change_points.usage_description: Identifies points where the underlying data generating process changes. Critical for understanding structural breaks, identifying when business conditions change, and detecting regime changes in time series data.

forecast_and_detect_anomalies.description: Forecast time series and detect anomalies based on forecast errors
forecast_and_detect_anomalies.usage_description: Uses forecasting models to predict expected values and identifies anomalies based on forecast errors. Useful for detecting unusual patterns in time series data and identifying deviations from expected trends.

batch_detect_anomalies.description: Apply multiple anomaly detection methods and combine results
batch_detect_anomalies.usage_description: Runs multiple anomaly detection algorithms and combines their results for more robust detection. Essential for comprehensive anomaly detection, reducing false positives, and improving detection accuracy through ensemble methods.
